title: Why Backflow Testing Still Matters: 57 U.S. Outbreaks and 9,734 Reported Illnesses
excerpt: EPA's historical outbreak and incident counts turn backflow from an abstract plumbing term into a real public-health risk that property owners can understand and act on.
audience: Commercial owners, HOAs, homeowners, facility managers

heroFact.label: EPA historical outbreak total
heroFact.context: EPA's cross-connection issue paper gives property owners a concrete benchmark for why backflow prevention is not just paperwork.

title: The Average Family Loses 9,400 Gallons a Year to Leaks - How Much Are You Wasting?
excerpt: Small leaks add up fast. EPA's current WaterSense figures make it easier to explain water waste in gallons homeowners can actually picture and budget around.

heroFact.value: 180 gallons per week; 9,400 gallons per year
heroFact.label: Average family leak waste
heroFact.context: EPA WaterSense gives a simple household benchmark that is easy for owners and landlords to understand.

title: Commercial Buildings Use 17% of Public-Water Withdrawals - Why Property Managers Should Track Water Like Energy
excerpt: Water is not a side issue for commercial buildings. EPA's 17% benchmark gives property managers a fast way to explain why leaks, fixture upgrades, and backflow compliance belong in the same operational conversation.
audience: Property managers, commercial owners, facility directors

heroFact.label: Commercial and institutional share of public-water withdrawals
heroFact.context: EPA WaterSense gives facility teams a clear benchmark for why water deserves the same management attention as energy and waste.
